name: Climb to the village of El Gasco — round trip through Las Hurdes
name_original: Subida al pueblo de El Gasco — ida y vuelta por Las Hurdes
region_intro: Road cycling around Herguijuela De La Sierra is characterized by a dramatically abrupt and diverse topography, featuring significant inclines and quartzite formations. The region is nestled within the Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, offering routes through lush forests of oak, chestnut, and pine. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from scenic valleys like Belén and Cabril to challenging climbs in the Sierra del Castillo, providing panoramic views. This area presents a compelling destination for road cyclists seeking varied and demanding routes. Best road cycling routes around Herguijuela De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Robledo from Las Mestas – Parque natural de Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia, a 35.8 miles (57.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alberca y Puerto del El Portillo - Batuercas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park — circular from Cepeda, a difficult 38.5 miles (62.0 km) path. This route offers sustained climbs and descents through the mountainous landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Villages of the Batuercas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park — circular from Sotoserrano, a 28.6 miles (46.1 km) trail leading through traditional villages and forested areas, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Herguijuela De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, diverse forests, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with challenging climbs and rewarding descents. The routes in Herguijuela De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Herguijuela De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Herguijuela de la Sierra?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes available around Herguijuela de la Sierra. The region is known for its challenging terrain, with 15 moderate and 32 difficult routes, making it ideal for cyclists seeking varied and demanding rides.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: Road cycling around Herguijuela de la Sierra features a dramatically abrupt and diverse topography with significant inclines and quartzite formations. You can expect sustained climbs and descents through mountainous landscapes, lush forests of oak, chestnut, and pine, and scenic valleys like Belén and Cabril. Routes often include challenging ascents in areas like the Sierra del Castillo, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, the region offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025214369/alberca-y-puerto-del-el-portillo-batuercas-sierra-de-francia-natural-park-circular-from-cepeda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberca y Puerto del El Portillo - Batuercas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park — circular from Cepeda&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 38.5-mile (62.0 km) path with significant elevation changes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025205986/villages-of-the-batuercas-sierra-de-francia-natural-park-circular-from-sotoserrano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villages of the Batuercas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park — circular from Sotoserrano&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.6-mile (46.1 km) route through traditional villages.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You can cycle towards high points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking panoramic vistas. Other routes might offer glimpses of the Alagón Valley or the Sierra de Candelario from elevated positions.

Q: Are there cultural attractions or traditional villages to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in cultural heritage. Many routes pass through or near charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mogarraz&lt;/a&gt;, La Alberca (with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/433119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town&lt;/a&gt;), and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda del Castañar&lt;/a&gt;, all declared Historic-Artistic Sites. These villages feature traditional architecture, narrow streets, and offer opportunities for cultural exploration and rest stops.

Q: When is the best time to visit Herguijuela de la Sierra for road cycling?
A: The region's diverse forests of oak, chestnut, pine, yew, holly, and alder trees provide a picturesque backdrop throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying the lush scenery. The varied topography means conditions can change with elevation, so checking local weather is always recommended.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: The road cycling routes around Herguijuela de la Sierra are predominantly moderate to difficult, characterized by mountainous terrain and significant inclines. Currently, there are no routes classified as 'easy' in our guide, meaning most options will present a challenge suitable for experienced road cyclists or those looking for a demanding ride rather than family-friendly leisure cycling.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Herguijuela de la Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Over 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Herguijuela de la Sierra's varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, rewarding descents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to look out for while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is home to some unique natural wonders. For instance, you might encounter the southernmost beech tree of Europe, a natural marvel over four hundred years old. The valleys of Belén, Cabril, and Las Esposas also offer distinct ecological characteristics and scenic beauty, with the Valley of Belén featuring the ruins of the Convent of Belén.

Q: Is there a route that offers a significant challenge for experienced road cyclists?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057565825/puerto-de-robledo-from-las-mestas-parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Robledo from Las Mestas – Parque natural de Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This difficult 35.8-mile (57.6 km) trail features substantial elevation gain through the natural park, requiring considerable endurance.

Q: Are there routes that explore the wider region, connecting multiple villages?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025176685/sotoserrano-lagunilla-and-montemayor-del-rio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotoserrano, Lagunilla, and Montemayor del Río Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a comprehensive exploration of the wider region. This difficult 36.9-mile (59.4 km) route connects several traditional villages, allowing cyclists to experience the local culture and diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that involve a notable climb?
A: Certainly. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain, and many routes feature significant climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025749407/climb-to-the-village-of-el-gasco-round-trip-through-las-hurdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the village of El Gasco — round trip through Las Hurdes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.6-mile (55.7 km) route specifically designed around a notable ascent, providing a rewarding challenge and scenic views.
tips: As the Ladrillar River passes through the town, a beautiful landscape of what is this area of the Batuercas Natural Park appears.
Views of the El Gasco meander

El Gasco is a farmhouse in the municipality of Nuñomoral and is nestled between mountains, on the banks of the Malvellido river. Nearby is the volcano, declared a Place of Scientific Interest in 2003. This has a 10-meter crater, inside which there is molten rock. Although traditionally it has been considered a volcano
Pretty town that is located in a deep valley at the passage of the Malvellido River. The slopes of the mountains fall to the foot of the town, not to be missed!
